revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近津神社 (小野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine |location = Aochō, Ono City, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1489 |ISO = |Position = {{coord|34|51|59.6|N|134|54|13.3|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} |Label Position = left |deity = {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |consecration_year = (Traditionally) 495 CE (8th year of Emperor Ninken) |Main Hall = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = October 17 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Chikatsu-jinja (Ono) - Torii 1.jpg|300px]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=51|lat_sec=59.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=54|lon_sec=13.3|label=Chikatsu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|qq=}} }} '''Chikatsu Shrine''' (近津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Aochō|ja|粟生町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aochō|1=Aochō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20037086}}, Ono City, Hyōgo Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine's Myōjin Torii was designated as a {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|ja|兵庫県指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵庫県指定文化財一覧|1=Hyōgo Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392892}} on March 24, 1972.<ref>[https://www.city.ono.hyogo.jp/1/4/4/1/1/7/ Chikatsu Shrine Myōjin Torii] (Ono City)</ref> == Overview == The principal deity of Chikatsu Shrine is {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, with subsidiary enshrined deities including {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. The shrine complex features a {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=honden|1=Honden}} (main hall), {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|qid=Q3125745|lt=haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} (worship hall), and stone {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} gates. Additional shrines within the grounds are dedicated to Toyōke Daijingū, Atago Shrine, Ōtoshi Shrine, and Akiba Shrine. The secondary Torii, a Myōjin Torii, is crafted from {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}}, standing 2.7 meters tall with pillars measuring 1.8 meters. The Torii is noted for its robust and archaic design, including entasis on the vertical supports and unique bracket construction.<ref>Hyōgo Prefecture Board of Education, "Prefectural Designated Cultural Property: Chikatsu Shrine Myōjin Torii," November 1990</ref> Inscribed on the front of the lintel is "Chikatsu Shrine," and on the reverse, "Crafted by master carpenter Fujiwara Jingorō in 1592 (20th year of Tenshō)." Despite some structural losses, the Torii remains a significant historical artifact. == History == Chikatsu Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in the 8th year of Emperor Ninken's reign (495 CE). According to the shrine's records, a golden-glowing tree appeared on a mountain during the day and night. When an imperial envoy visited the site, an old man claiming to be an incarnation of the Great Sun Buddha appeared, requesting the establishment of a shrine. This led to the construction of the shrine at the foot of the mountain in March of the same year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/978702|title=Kato District Records|accessdate=2020-03-24|publisher=National Diet Library Digital Collection|pages=607-608|qq=}}</ref> The shrine suffered destruction during the Genkō period (1331–1333) but was later rebuilt. In the Chōroku era (1457–1459), the regional lord Akamatsu Masanori prayed for his clan's prosperity at the shrine and subsequently renovated the complex. During the Tenshō era (1573–1592), the shrine played a role in Toyotomi Hideyoshi's campaign against Bessho Nagaharu at {{ill|Miki Castle|en|Miki Castle|qid=Q11242473|lt=Miki Castle|1=Miki Castle}}, leading to the grant of shrine lands after the castle fell. In the early Edo period (Kan'ei era, 1624–1643), the shrine received additional land grants, which were upheld by successive lords of the Himeji domain. In 1874, the shrine was formally designated a village shrine. ; Former Affiliate: Takana-yama Jingū-ji Hōshōbō Jigan'in Prior to the Meiji era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Chikatsu Shrine was associated with a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple, Jigan'in. Its central deity, Buddha Vairocana, was worshiped as the essence of the shrine's main deity.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/978702|title=Kato District Records|accessdate=2020-03-24|publisher=National Diet Library Digital Collection|qq=}}</ref> == Other Features == Chikatsu Shrine is located at the foot of the Aonogahara Plateau. The precincts house deteriorating historical ema (votive plaques), including one depicting the "O-Kage Dance" (designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Ono City in 1831).<ref>[https://www.city.ono.hyogo.jp/1/4/4/1/1/36-copy/ Chikatsu Shrine O-Kage Dance Ema] (Ono City)</ref> == Address == * 1489 Aochō, Ono City, Hyōgo Prefecture, 675-1358 == Access == * 14-minute walk (1.1 km) from {{ill|Ao Station|en|Ao Station|qid=Q1104653|lt=Aō Station|1=Ao Station}} (JR Kakogawa Line, Kobe Electric Railway) * 25-minute walk (2 km) from Kawai-nishi Station (JR Kakogawa Line) * 4-minute walk (268 m) from Chikatsu Shrine Mae bus stop == Nearby Landmarks and Facilities == * {{ill|Kawakami Castle|ja|金鑵城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Castle|1=Kanatsurube Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110853291}} Ruins * {{ill|Kawai West Temple Site|ja|河合西廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai West Temple Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|qid=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} Historic Sites * {{ill|Onogahara Plateau|ja|青野ヶ原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onogahara Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Footnotes Help|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|qid=Q460367|lt=Todai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} Lands * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|日本の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines|1=list of Shinto shrines in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11506049}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/63dd043.html Chikatsu Shrine on Hyōgo Shrine Office Website] * [https://yaokami.jp/1281894/ Chikatsu Shrine on Yaokami.jp] * [http://www.shintetsu.co.jp/sp/railway/guide/odekake/article.php?id=181 Chikatsu Shrine on Kobe Electric Railway Guide] {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
